title: Introduction
description: NOC 64322 – Outdoor sport and recreational guides | GTR Immigration NOC 64322 (Outdoor sport and recreational guides) includes the specialized leaders of Canada’s world-class adventure tourism sector. From mountain climbing and white-water rafting to hunting and fishing expeditions, these TEER 4 professionals ensure both the safety and the thrill of the Canadian wilderness. For immigration, this role is a "high-priority" category for the BC PNP and Alberta Opportunity Stream, especially for those with high-level technical certifications in safety and rescue.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level4Major Group64 – Sales and service representativesSub-Major Group643 – Occupations in food, accommodation and tourismMinor Group6432 – Tourism and amusement services occupationsNOC Code64322

duties: Plan and lead expeditions and provide instruction in outdoor activities such as climbing, rafting, or canoeing.
Advise on safety measures and ensure that all participants follow environmental and safety regulations.
Assemble, maintain, and repair necessary outdoor equipment and supplies.
Provide first aid and emergency assistance in remote locations when necessary.
Narrate facts about the history, flora, and fauna of the specific region to enhance the guest experience.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Demonstrated high-level ability in a specific outdoor sport is mandatory.
Specialized certifications (e.g., Wilderness First Responder, Swiftwater Rescue, or specific sport instruction) are almost always required.
Several years of experience in the specific outdoor activity are necessary for senior guiding roles.
job_outlook: The outlook for outdoor guides is Favourable. As global travelers increasingly prioritize "experience-based" and "eco-friendly" travel, the requirement for professional, safety-oriented guides will continue to grow across Canada's remote and protected regions.
labour_market_demand: The demand for outdoor guides is heavily seasonal and geographically concentrated in the Rockies (AB/BC), the North, and Atlantic Canada. With the 2026 expansion of eco-tourism initiatives, there is a specific shortage of guides with advanced wilderness first aid and specialized technical skills (e.g., UIAGM/IFMGA or ACMG certification).
World-Class Landscapes: Lead expeditions in some of the globe's most iconic national parks like Banff, Jasper, and Whistler.
Adventure Tourism Growth: Canada is a global leader in sustainable adventure travel, ensuring a steady stream of international clients.
Community and Lifestyle: Ideal for those seeking an active, outdoor-centric life in vibrant, close-knit mountain or coastal communities.
pathways: BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled: A dedicated stream for those working in the tourism/hospitality sectors (requires 9 months of work in BC).
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Fast-track for those already working in Alberta in this category with a valid job offer.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A direct route for TEER 4 workers with job offers in the Maritimes.
CEC (Canadian Experience Class): Accessible after completing 12 months of full-time work in Canada.
